ExecPolicyAmendment.description:
Proposed execpolicy change to allow commands starting with this prefix.

The `command` tokens form the prefix that would be added as an execpolicy
`prefix_rule(..., decision="allow")`, letting the agent bypass approval for
commands that start with this token sequence.

ReviewDecision.description: User's decision in response to an ExecApprovalRequest.

oneOf.description: User has approved this command and the agent should execute it.

User has approved this command and wants to apply the proposed execpolicy
amendment so future matching commands are permitted.
oneOf.title: ApprovedExecpolicyAmendmentReviewDecision

oneOf.description:
User has approved this request and wants future prompts in the same
session-scoped approval cache to be automatically approved for the
remainder of the session.

User chose to persist a network policy rule (allow/deny) for future
requests to the same host.
oneOf.title: NetworkPolicyAmendmentReviewDecision

oneOf.description:
User has denied this command and the agent should not execute it, but
it should continue the session and try something else.

The approval flow timed out before the user or guardian completed a
decision. Downstream may surface this distinctly from an explicit
denial so agents can retry or explain the timeout accurately.

User has denied this command and the agent should not do anything until
the user's next command.
